name: N-naturalness
summary: Postulate N copies of the Standard Model with Higgs mass-squareds spread over a range; only the copy with the smallest positive m_H^2 reheats efficiently after inflation, becoming our visible sector. Provides a mechanism for the EW hierarchy without low-scale new physics, by selecting the most-tuned sector cosmologically.

addresses_puzzles.note: Reduces tuning to ~1/N rather than M_Planck^2/m_H^2; for N ~ 10^{16} the hierarchy is explained without TeV-scale partners.

touches_benchmarks.note: Other reheated sectors contribute to N_eff; viable variants require asymmetric reheating or specific decay channels.

new_fields: N - 1 copies of the full SM
introduces.note: Reheaton coupling, mass distribution width, total N, and inflationary scale.

history: Arkani-Hamed, Cohen, D'Agnolo, Hook, Kim, Pinner (2016). Builds on neutral-naturalness ideas (twin Higgs) by replacing two sectors with N >> 1 sectors and a cosmological selection mechanism.

references.citation: Arkani-Hamed, Cohen, D'Agnolo, Hook, Kim, Pinner, 'Solving the hierarchy problem at reheating with a large number of degrees of freedom', PRL 117, 251801 (2016)
